message:
test(ondisk): add four ext4 extent-block checksum sensitivity / detection relations (bd-cigna)

Apply the same MR template the inode (bd-z75f6) and dir-block (bd-anfg0)
checksum harnesses now use to the third leg of the ext4 metadata-checksum
trio: extent tree blocks.  Pre-existing coverage was the standard
no-panic / short-rejects / round-trip set, which confirms the digest is
stamp/verify-symmetric but not that it actually catches replay or
single-bit corruption.

New cases (all in crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs):

- ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_is_sensitive_to_inode_number — same
  body, two ino numbers ⇒ different stored checksum bytes; cross-slot
  verify must fail (defends against extent-tree-block replay between
  inodes).
- ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_is_sensitive_to_generation —
  generation flip shifts the stored checksum (the per-inode seed mixes
  in `generation`, same NFS-handle anti-replay mechanism as inode/dir).
- ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_is_sensitive_to_csum_seed —
  cross-seed stamps differ; cross-seed verify fails (defends against
  cross-filesystem stamp replay).
- ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_detects_single_bit_flip_in_body —
  any bit flip in the covered prefix [0..tail_off] invalidates verify;
  flips into the 4-byte stored-checksum slot are correctly excluded via
  prop_assume! (the verifier reads — not CRCs — those bytes, so
  flipping there changes which value the comparator reads but is not
  the property MR-DETECT pins down).

Adds a `build_extent_block_for_checksum_test(eh_max)` helper next to the
dir-block one so the four cases share a single, well-formed extent
header + ramp body + zero tail setup.  The existing round-trip test is
left untouched (still computes its checksum manually) to keep the diff
focused on net-new behavioural coverage.

All 5 ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_* cases pass under
cargo test -p ffs-ondisk; clippy --tests -- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
